title: General
text: Read DESCRIPTION, README Red/green TDD

title: General
text: Literal, direct, concise, high-signal, non-empathic. hedging, -sidesing, closing summaries, offers. ask questions functionally blocked.

title: General
text: manual edits: .Rd, NAMESPACE Use devtools::document(), test(), check() Deps: prefer Base R current closure. Permission required new deps make code better Add deps via usethis::use_import_from() use_package() air format . jarl check . --fix --allow-dirty devtools::document() devtools::check() Report covr::package_coverage()

title: Authors and Citation

title: Authors and Citation
text: Srimath Kandali V (2026). ascribe: Static AST Scanning R Package Function Usage. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://ascribe.visruth.com.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ascribe: Static AST Scanning of R Package and Function Usage},   author = {Visruth {Srimath Kandali}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://ascribe.visruth.com}, }

what: Static AST Scanning of R Package and Function Usage
title: Static AST Scanning of R Package and Function Usage
text: ascribe scans R projects package function use. packages can use results generate citations without maintaining parser.

title: Static AST Scanning of R Package and Function Usage
text: can install development version ascribe GitHub :

title: Static AST Scanning of R Package and Function Usage
text: Build package universe, cite code uses :
code: library(ascribe)  universe <- build_universe_data("stats") usage <- scan_usage(path, universe$packages, universe$export_index, universe$origin_map) cite_usage(usage)

what: ascribe: Static AST Scanning of R Package and Function Usage — ascribe-package
title: ascribe: Static AST Scanning of R Package and Function Usage — ascribe-package
text: Scans R source files package function use, builds citations configurable package universes. Supports .R, .Rmd, .qmd files, resolves unqualified calls attachment order re-export origin, leaves package collection define citations. See stanflow example usage.

title: ascribe: Static AST Scanning of R Package and Function Usage — ascribe-package
text: Maintainer: Visruth Srimath Kandali public@visruth.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Visruth Srimath Kandali public@visruth.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Build an inverted export index — build_export_index
title: Build an inverted export index — build_export_index
text: Given named list mapping package names character vectors function names (produced collect_pkg_funs()), creates inverted index mapping function names character vectors packages export .

title: Build an inverted export index — build_export_index
Build an inverted export index — build_export_index
text: exports Named list. Names package names, values character vectors function names.

title: Build an inverted export index — build_export_index
text: Named list mapping function names character vectors package names.

title: Build an inverted export index — build_export_index
code: exports <- list(   pkgA = c("foo", "bar"),   pkgB = c("foo", "baz") ) build_export_index(exports) #> $bar #> [1] "pkgA" #>  #> $baz #> [1] "pkgB" #>  #> $foo #> [1] "pkgA" "pkgB" #>

what: Build an origin map for package functions — build_origin_map
title: Build an origin map for package functions — build_origin_map
text: Given named list mapping package names character vectors function names, creates named character vector mapping "pkg::fun" keys origin package. Functions whose origin determined fall back providing package.

title: Build an origin map for package functions — build_origin_map
Build an origin map for package functions — build_origin_map
text: exports Named list. Names package names, values character vectors function names.

title: Build an origin map for package functions — build_origin_map
text: Named character vector mapping "pkg::fun" origin package.

title: Build an origin map for package functions — build_origin_map
code: exports <- list(   stats = collect_pkg_funs("stats"),   utils = collect_pkg_funs("utils") ) build_origin_map(exports) #>                     stats::asOneSidedFormula  #> what: Build scanner data for a package universe — build_universe_data
title: Build scanner data for a package universe — build_universe_data
text: Given character vector package names, computes export lists, inverted export index, origin map, version snapshot needed scan_usage(). packages must installed.

title: Build scanner data for a package universe — build_universe_data
Build scanner data for a package universe — build_universe_data
text: packages Character vector package names.

title: Build scanner data for a package universe — build_universe_data
text: named list components: packages input package names. exports Named list mapping package names character vectors exported function names (collect_pkg_funs()). export_index Named list mapping function names character vectors packages (build_export_index()). origin_map Named character vector mapping "pkg::fun" keys origin packages (build_origin_map()). pkg_versions Named list mapping package names version strings.

title: Build scanner data for a package universe — build_universe_data

what: Cite package and function use in a project — cite_usage
title: Cite package and function use in a project — cite_usage
text: Builds citations scan_usage() results. Package collections supply citation records package-citation policy.

title: Cite package and function use in a project — cite_usage
code: cite_usage(   usage,   package_citations = list(),   function_citations = list(),   package_citation = utils::citation,   always_cite = character(),   format = c("bibtex", "bibentry") )

title: Cite package and function use in a project — cite_usage
text: usage Results returned scan_usage(). package_citations named list environment package citation entries. Missing packages use package_citation. function_citations named list environment function citation entries, keyed "pkg::function". package_citation function accepts package name returns citation entries. Defaults utils::citation(). always_cite Character vector packages cite addition packages found scan. format One "bibtex" "bibentry".

title: Cite package and function use in a project — cite_usage
text: BibTeX character vector bibentry object.

title: Cite package and function use in a project — cite_usage
code: path <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines("stats::median(1:3)", path) universe <- build_universe_data(c("stats", "tools")) usage <- scan_usage(path, universe$packages, universe$export_index, universe$origin_map) #> ℹ Searching /tmp/RtmpSTQqH6/file191340bea492.R cite_usage(usage) #> @Manual{, #>   title = {R: A Language and Environment for Statistical Computing}, #>   author = {{R Core Team}}, #>   organization = {R Foundation for Statistical Computing}, #>   address = {Vienna, Austria}, #>   year = {2026}, #>   doi = {10.32614/R.manuals}, #>   url = {https://www.R-project.org/}, #> } #>  #> @Manual{, #>   title = {R: A Language and Environment for Statistical Computing}, #>   author = {{R Core Team}}, #>   organization = {R Foundation for Statistical Computing}, #>   address = {Vienna, Austria}, #>   year = {2026}, #>   doi = {10.32614/R.manuals}, #>   url = {https://www.R-project.org/}, #> } unlink(path)

what: Collect exported functions and R6 methods from a package — collect_pkg_funs
title: Collect exported functions and R6 methods from a package — collect_pkg_funs
text: Returns character vector function names exported pkg, including methods exported namespace-internal R6 classes.

title: Collect exported functions and R6 methods from a package — collect_pkg_funs
Collect exported functions and R6 methods from a package — collect_pkg_funs
text: pkg Package name (character scalar).

title: Collect exported functions and R6 methods from a package — collect_pkg_funs
text: Character vector function/method names.

title: Collect exported functions and R6 methods from a package — collect_pkg_funs

what: Collect R6 class method names from a package — collect_r6_methods
title: Collect R6 class method names from a package — collect_r6_methods
text: Scans exported objects namespace-internal objects R6 class generators, collects public method names.

title: Collect R6 class method names from a package — collect_r6_methods
Collect R6 class method names from a package — collect_r6_methods
text: pkg Package name (character scalar). export_names Character vector exported names (getNamespaceExports()).

title: Collect R6 class method names from a package — collect_r6_methods
text: Character vector R6 method names.

title: Collect R6 class method names from a package — collect_r6_methods

what: Generate sysdata.rda for a package universe — generate_universe_sysdata
title: Generate sysdata.rda for a package universe — generate_universe_sysdata
text: Computes scanner data given packages saves sysdata.rda variable names prefixed prefix. intended use downstream package's data-raw/sysdata.R script.

title: Generate sysdata.rda for a package universe — generate_universe_sysdata
code: generate_universe_sysdata(   packages,   prefix,   extra_vars = list(),   include_scanner_defaults = FALSE,   file = "R/sysdata.rda" )

title: Generate sysdata.rda for a package universe — generate_universe_sysdata
text: packages Character vector package names. prefix Character scalar used name saved objects (e.g., "stan" produces .stan_pkgs). extra_vars Named list additional objects include saved file (e.g., citation environments). include_scanner_defaults TRUE, also saves .stdlib_funs .scan_skip_dirs. Defaults FALSE. file Output path. Defaults "R/sysdata.rda".

title: Generate sysdata.rda for a package universe — generate_universe_sysdata
text: Invisibly returns result build_universe_data().

title: Generate sysdata.rda for a package universe — generate_universe_sysdata
text: generated variables : .{prefix}_pkgs Character vector package names. .{prefix}_exports Named list exported functions per package. .{prefix}_export_index Inverted index: function name packages. .{prefix}_origin_map Named character vector: "pkg::fun" origin. .{prefix}_pkg_versions Named list version strings. include_scanner_defaults TRUE, .stdlib_funs .scan_skip_dirs also saved.

title: Generate sysdata.rda for a package universe — generate_universe_sysdata
code: file <- tempfile(fileext = ".rda") generate_universe_sysdata(c("stats", "utils"), "my", file = file) #> ✔ Successfully generated /tmp/RtmpSTQqH6/file19137dcea3b3.rda unlink(file)

what: Ignored functions/directories used by scanner — internal_data
title: Ignored functions/directories used by scanner — internal_data
text: Vector functions ignored parsing. Generated data-raw/sysdata.R exports base R packages. Vector directories skipped recursively searching project. Generated data-raw/sysdata.R.

title: Ignored functions/directories used by scanner — internal_data
Ignored functions/directories used by scanner — internal_data

what: Resolve the origin package of an exported function — resolve_origin
title: Resolve the origin package of an exported function — resolve_origin
text: Given package function name, determines package function actually originates (handling re-exports).

title: Resolve the origin package of an exported function — resolve_origin
Resolve the origin package of an exported function — resolve_origin
text: pkg Package name (character scalar). name Function name (character scalar).

title: Resolve the origin package of an exported function — resolve_origin
text: origin package name, NA_character_ undetermined.

title: Resolve the origin package of an exported function — resolve_origin

what: Find used functions and packages — scan_usage
title: Find used functions and packages — scan_usage
text: Statically scans R source files package attachments function calls. recognizes library(), require(), requireNamespace(), use().

title: Find used functions and packages — scan_usage
code: scan_usage(   path = ".",   allowed_packages,   export_index,   origin_map,   ignore_unqualified_functions = .stdlib_funs,   strict = FALSE,   skip_dirs = .scan_skip_dirs,   metapackages = NULL,   use_knitr = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Find used functions and packages — scan_usage
text: path single project directory (searched recursively) vector files (.R/.Rmd/.qmd). allowed_packages Character vector package namespaces attribute calls . export_index Named list mapping function names packages. origin_map Named character vector mapping pkg::fun keys origin package. ignore_unqualified_functions Defaults exports base R packages listed stdlib_funs(). Character vector function names ignore attributing (unqualified) calls. Calls like pkg::fun() ignored even fun ignore_unqualified_functions, since namespaced. strict TRUE (default), count unqualified function calls whose origin can determined exactly static scan, including attachment-order tie-breaks winner unambiguous file. Unresolved calls warned omitted. skip_dirs Character vector directory names skip scanning directory. Defaults scan_skip_dirs(). metapackages Named list mapping attached package names additional packages treated co-attached unqualified resolution. Defaults NULL. use_knitr Logical. TRUE, parse .Rmd .qmd files knitr::purl(). accurate knitr/quarto chunk handling much slower default -house parser. Defaults FALSE. quiet Logical. TRUE, suppresses status messages. Defaults FALSE.

title: Find used functions and packages — scan_usage
text: list packages, resolved functions, ambiguous function calls.

title: Find used functions and packages — scan_usage
text: Explicit package references library(), require(), requireNamespace(), use(), pkg::fun recorded package included allowed_packages. scanner attributes unqualified function library() require() attached package earlier file supplied indexes can resolve call. metapackages can add packages attachment set. several attached packages export function, recently attached match wins. scanner attributes known re-exports origin package otherwise resolved package.

title: Find used functions and packages — scan_usage
code: path <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines(   c(     "# one messy analysis file",     "library(stats)",     "requireNamespace(\"utils\")",     "filter(1:10, rep(1, 3))",     "utils::head(letters)"   ),   path ) scan_usage(   path,   allowed_packages = c("stats", "utils"),   export_index = list(filter = "stats"),   origin_map = c("stats::filter" = "stats"),   ignore_unqualified_functions = character(),   quiet = TRUE ) #> $packages #> [1] "stats" "utils" #>  #> $functions #> [1] "stats::filter" "utils::head"   #>  #> $ambiguous #> character(0) #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "scan_usage" unlink(path)
